hypothesis: If task-tool semantic alignment (measured by higher embedding cosine similarity or via augmentation with a hypothesis-generator tool) is increased, then scientific LLM agents will achieve larger improvements in success rate and pass@k metrics across environments than they do from switching agent scaffolds (ReAct vs tool-calling) or LLM backends (Claude-3.5 Sonnet vs GPT-4o), because aligned tools better enable correct task decomposition and effective tool invocation.
